qa_pairs.question: What steps should an Academic Principal Investigator (PI) follow to submit their Canadian Common CV (CCV) when applying for a Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) through the Digital Research Alliance of Canada?
qa_pairs.answer: An Academic PI should navigate to the Resource Application page on CCDB, go to the 'Ongoing Competitions' tab, and click 'Apply' or 'Edit' for their application. Within the application form, they must scroll to the 'Canadian Common CV' section, enter the submission confirmation number obtained from their Alliance template CCV, and then click 'Submit CCV'. After submission, they must also report contributions enabled by computational resources as per Section 6 of the document. If a sponsored user is delegating the application, they should follow these same steps on behalf of the PI.

qa_pairs.question: What are the requirements for Co-Principal Investigators (Co-PIs) regarding their Canadian Common CV (CCV) when part of a Digital Research Alliance of Canada Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application, and how can they update it?
qa_pairs.answer: All Co-PIs on a RAC application must have an updated Canadian Common CV (CCV) on file, submitted within the last 90 days. The Academic PI will be unable to submit the RAC application until all Co-PIs meet this requirement. Co-PIs can update their CCV by either navigating to the application where they are listed 'As a Co-PI of' on the 'Resource Applications' page and clicking 'Update CCV', or by going to the 'My Account' menu and clicking 'View reporting' on the Reporting page.

qa_pairs.question: When an Academic Principal Investigator submits a new Canadian Common CV (CCV) for a Digital Research Alliance of Canada Resource Allocation Competition, how do they report publications that were enabled by the Alliance's computational resources?
qa_pairs.answer: When a new CCV is uploaded, publications from the Alliance CCV template will appear in the 'CCV Publications Reporting' section of the CCV submission form on CCDB. Publications for which an answer has not yet been provided will be surrounded by a red border, and all must be addressed to submit the RAC application. The PI can check the box next to each publication to mark it as enabled by resources, or use 'Select all new' to mark all new publications. To mark a publication as *not* enabled by resources, they must click it twice: the first click indicates it was enabled, and the second click removes the checkmark, indicating it was not enabled.
